facts.fact: Snowflake reports 12,062 global customers on its About page.
Snowflake offers four pricing editions: Standard, Enterprise, Business Critical, and Virtual Private Snowflake (VPS).
Per-credit pricing (AWS, US East Northern Virginia) is $2.00 for Standard, $3.00 for Enterprise, and $4.00 for Business Critical; VPS pricing requires contacting Sales.
On-demand storage is priced at $23.00 per TB per month (AWS, US East Northern Virginia), billed on average monthly storage after compression.
Snowflake uses a consumption-based pricing model, with two ways to buy: On-demand or pre-paid capacity.
Business Critical edition adds features like Tri-Secret Secure, private connectivity access, and failover/failback for disaster recovery, intended for highly regulated industries.
Snowflake's free trial offers a 30-day trial with $400 in free credits.
Snowflake is a fully managed service with serverless capabilities; users configure virtual warehouses while Snowflake handles infrastructure, scaling, and maintenance.
Snowflake's platform spans four benefit areas/workloads: Data Engineering, Analytics, AI, and Applications & Collaboration.

Standard Warehouse - Generation 2 (Gen2) delivers about 2.0x faster performance for core analytics workloads, and Snowflake Adaptive Compute auto-selects right-sized compute with intelligent query routing.
Snowflake Horizon Catalog provides security and governance, including read/write access for all Iceberg tables in open table formats.
Snowflake reports more than 6,780 employees across over 40 offices worldwide (as of October 2023).
The AT&T case study cites 84% savings on estimated annual costs (via results caching) and under 1 second to answer 90% of user queries.
